symbols: cross shape formed by light — the Cross of Christ as instrument of salvation and cosmic axis
radiating light — divine glory, Christ as the Light of the World (JHN.8.12)
pink-magenta glow — the blending of blood (red) and purity (white), alluding to sacrificial atonement
yellow-gold light — divine glory and heavenly radiance, the uncreated light of God
corner placement — the cornerstone rejected by builders, set in the angle of two walls (PSA.118.22)
description: A glowing cross formed by fluorescent light tubes is mounted in the corner where two yellow walls meet. A horizontal yellow tube intersects a vertical dark fixture, casting a vivid pink-magenta radiance outward in a four-pointed pattern against the warm golden-yellow walls. The glow softens toward the edges, creating a luminous halo effect around the cross form. The floor below is bare and pale.
theological_description: Though a work of contemporary minimalism, the cruciform light installation evokes profound theological resonance. The cross radiating light recalls Christ's declaration 'I am the light of the world' (JHN.8.12) and the Johannine prologue's assertion that 'the light shines in the darkness, and the darkness has not overcome it' (JHN.1.5). The placement in a corner — where two planes meet — suggests the cornerstone imagery of Psalm 118:22 and Ephesians 2:20, Christ as the junction point uniting what was divided. The pink-magenta radiance bleeding outward from the cross evokes the blood and water flowing from Christ's pierced side (JHN.19.34), transformed here into pure light — death transfigured into glory. Patristically, the Fathers spoke of the Cross as the 'tree of light' that reversed the tree of the Fall. Gregory of Nyssa and Pseudo-Dionysius both describe the divine light as simultaneously blinding and illuminating, accessible yet transcendent — precisely the paradox this glowing form embodies. The bare simplicity strips away narrative to present the Cross as a pure theological sign: the axis mundi where heaven meets earth.

scripture_refs.reason: Christ as the Light of the World — the cross radiates light as the source of spiritual illumination
The light shines in the darkness — the luminous cross glowing against the surrounding space
Boasting only in the Cross of Christ — the cross as sole focal point and source of all radiance
The cornerstone — the cross placed in the corner where two walls meet
Christ on the Cross reconciling two into one — the cross uniting the two wall planes
Blood and water from Christ's side — the pink-red light emanating from the cross form
The Lamb is the lamp of the New Jerusalem — the cross as source of light needing no other illumination
